register: documentary, camera-objective narration; humor arises from events and dialogue only, never from the narrator
notes: the narrator is a transparent witness: no interpretation, no metaphor, no personification, no judgment
dialogue carries the scenes; narration stages and records
animals are people-shaped in behavior; do not over-explain the magic

naming.rule: Two registers. Fable-named animals are capitalized as personal names: Cat and Fox take no article; the Firebird and the King keep the definite article (unique/titular beings); direct address drops the article. Humans carry personal names (Mila, Jara). Relational nouns stay common and lowercase: 'her aunt', 'the guards'. One form per character, everywhere in narration.

anatomy.rule: Anthropomorphic convention: the grasping extremity is a paw, never a hand. No fingers, knuckles, or fists - use claws, pads, toes, or 'tightened his grip'. Arms vs forelegs follows posture in the moment: bipedal action allows arms; quadruped motion uses forelegs and hind legs. Shared anatomy (shoulders, chest, jaw, ears, tail) is always fine. Humans are humans. Birds have wings and talons, never arms or paws.
